source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/라우터 (Archer C64)/10. Test Lab ISO 17025 Certificate/ISO_IEC 17025_Test laboratory_BACL.pdf

document.title: ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408
standards: Canada Consumer Product Safety Act, Toys Regulations SOR/2011-17(2019)
Canada Consumer Product Safety Act, Toys Regulations SOR/2011-17(2022)
ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Computers V8.0
ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Displays V8.0
ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Water Coolers V3.0

sourceSummary.oneLine: Final pages of CNAS L2408 ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation scope list accredited toy safety, electric toy, food contact, and ENERGY STAR non-lighting test methods with effective dates.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as laboratory qualification evidence showing which standards and clauses are within the accredited scope; verify relevance before using for router or FDA submission files because this chunk is mainly consumer products, food contact, and energy-efficiency testing.
keyPoints: The certificate pages state that the Chinese accreditation scope is the definitive version.

The scope includes extensive toy mechanical and physical safety testing under EN 71-1, ASTM F963-23, CPSC 16 CFR, Canadian Toys Regulations, AS/NZS ISO 8124.1, Japan ST 2016-1, and China GB standards.
The scope includes electric toy safety testing under EN/IEC/BS/AS-NZS 62115, GB 19865-2005, and 16 CFR Part 1505.
The scope includes food-contact material tests, including migration, lead/cadmium release, extractives, specific gravity, melting point, pentachlorophenol, wipe metals, and formaldehyde methods.
The scope includes ENERGY STAR non-lighting product tests for computers, power supplies, servers, imaging equipment, telephony, displays, audio/video, and water coolers.

entities.name: CNAS
entities.evidence: Header identifies an ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation certificate, No. CNAS L2408.

entities.name: CPSC
entities.evidence: Multiple rows cite US Code of Federal Regulation CPSC 16 CFR provisions for toys and flammability.

entities.name: US FDA
entities.evidence: Food contact material scope cites US FDA 21 CFR 176.170, 175.300, and multiple 177-series sections.

entities.name: Health Canada Product Safety Laboratory
entities.evidence: Rows cite Health Canada Product Safety Laboratory Reference Manual Book 5 test methods for small components, sharp edges, sharp points, eye/nose security, sound level, and rattles.

entities.name: ENERGY STAR
entities.evidence: Section 七 lists ENERGY STAR(Non-Lighting Products) and program requirements/test methods for multiple product categories.

entities.name: ASTM International
entities.evidence: Rows cite ASTM F963-23 toy safety and ASTM F2923-20 children's jewelry standards.

concepts.name: ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation scope
concepts.evidence: Each page header states ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书, No. CNAS L2408, with test objects, standards or methods, notes, and effective dates.

concepts.name: Toy mechanical and physical safety testing
concepts.evidence: Scope lists toy tests such as small parts, sharp edges, impact, torque, tension, acoustic requirements, projectiles, and markings under EN 71-1, ASTM F963-23, AS/NZS ISO 8124.1, ST 2016-1, and GB 6675.1-2014.

concepts.name: Toy flammability testing
concepts.evidence: Scope lists flammability requirements and test methods under GB 6675.3-2014, ISO 8124-2:2014, EN 71-2:2020, ASTM F963-23, CPSC 16 CFR 1500.44 and 1610, Canadian Toys Regulations, AS/NZS 8124.2:2016, and ST 2016-2.

concepts.name: Electric toy safety testing
concepts.evidence: Scope lists electric toy marking, power input, heating, electric strength, mechanical strength, construction, components, and heat/fire tests under EN IEC 62115, BS EN IEC 62115, AS/NZS 62115, IEC 62115, GB 19865-2005, and 16 CFR Part 1505.

concepts.name: Food contact material compliance testing
concepts.evidence: Food containers and food contact materials section lists volatile organic matter, sensory examination, migration, lead/cadmium release, extractives, specific gravity, melting point, pentachlorophenol, wipe lead/cadmium, and formaldehyde methods.

concepts.name: ENERGY STAR non-lighting product testing
concepts.evidence: ENERGY STAR section lists computers, internal AC-DC/DC-DC power supplies, computer servers, imaging equipment, telephony, displays, audio/video, and water coolers.

evidence.claim: The excerpt is from an ISO/IEC 17025 accreditation certificate scope for CNAS L2408.
evidence.evidenceText: Header repeatedly states “ISO/IEC 17025 认可证书 No. CNAS L2408” and “The scope of the accreditation in Chinese remains the definitive version.”

evidence.claim: The accredited toy mechanical/physical scope includes EN 71-1:2014+A1:2018 and BS EN 71-1:2014+A1:2018 clauses for hazards and test methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list EN 71-1/BS EN 71-1 items such as small balls, suction cups, warnings, small parts cylinder, torque test, tension test, impact test, and projectile tests with effective date 2023-04-06.

evidence.claim: The scope covers Canadian toy regulation requirements under SOR/2011-17 in both 2019 and 2022 versions for multiple toy safety topics.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows cite Canada Consumer Product Safety Act, Toys Regulations SOR/2011-17(2019) and SOR/2011-17(2022) for official languages, flexible film bags, small parts, edges, projectiles, stability, decibel limit, dolls/plush toys, rattles, and Schedule 4.

evidence.claim: The scope includes ASTM F963-23 toy safety requirements and test methods effective 2024-04-16, with stated exclusions for selected clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: ASTM F963-23 rows include material quality, stuffing materials, sound-producing toys, small objects except 4.6.2 mouth-actuated toys, battery-operated toys except 4.25.10, abuse testing, impact, torque, tension, stalled motor, noise, and expanding materials.

evidence.claim: The scope includes U.S. CPSC toy regulations and test methods under 16 CFR parts 1500, 1501, and 1510.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list CPSC 16 CFR 1500.19, 1500.48, 1500.49, 1500.50, 1500.51, 1500.52, 1500.53, 16 CFR 1501, and 16 CFR 1510 for misbranding, sharp points/edges, use-and-abuse simulation, small-parts hazards, and rattles.

evidence.claim: The scope includes Health Canada Product Safety Laboratory toy test methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows cite Health Canada Product Safety Laboratory Reference Manual Book 5 methods M-00.1 small components, M-00.2 sharp edges, M-00.3 sharp points, M-00.4 eye/nose security, M-01.1 reasonably foreseeable use, M-03 flexible film bags, M-04 sound level, and M-05 rattles.

evidence.claim: The accredited scope includes AS/NZS ISO 8124.1:2023 toy mechanical and physical property requirements and methods, often paired with the 2019+A1+A2 edition.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list AS/NZS ISO 8124.1:2023 for normal use, foreseeable abuse, material, small parts, edges, cords, enclosures, projectile toys, acoustic requirements, labeling, instructional literature, manufacturer markings, and toy gun marking.

evidence.claim: The scope includes Japan ST 2016-1 toy mechanical and physical property requirements and test methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list ST 2016-1 clauses for normal use, small parts, edges, cords, enclosures, projectile toys, batteries, acoustic requirements, packaging, marking, sharp-edge test, sharp-point test, and sound pressure levels.

evidence.claim: The scope includes China GB toy standards for basic code and flammability.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows cite GB 6675.1-2014 for mechanical and physical properties, flammability, electrical, hygiene requirements, and toy marking; GB 6675.3-2014 for flammability requirements and tests.

evidence.claim: The flammability scope covers ISO 8124-2:2014, EN 71-2:2020, BS EN 71-2:2020, ASTM F963-23, CPSC, Canadian, AS/NZS, and Japan ST methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list ISO 8124-2:2014, EN/BS EN 71-2:2020, ASTM F963-23 section 4.2 and annexes A5/A6, 16 CFR 1500.44, 16 CFR 1610, Canadian Toys Regulations flammability clauses, AS/NZS 8124.2:2016, and ST 2016-2.

evidence.claim: The scope includes children's jewelry safety tests under ASTM F2923-20.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list ASTM F2923-20 for breakaway features and releases, sharp points/edges/use and abuse, small parts, cautionary labeling, batteries, and breakaway tension test.

evidence.claim: The electric toy safety scope includes EN IEC 62115:2020+A11:2020, BS EN IEC 62115:2020+A11:2020, AS/NZS 62115:2018 Amd 1:2021, IEC 62115:2017, GB 19865-2005, and 16 CFR Part 1505.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list electric toy marking, power input, heating and abnormal operation, electric strength, liquid/water use, mechanical strength, construction, cords/wires, components, screws, clearances, resistance to heat/fire, labeling, electrical design, performance, and temperature limits.

evidence.claim: The food-contact material scope includes migration and chemical release tests under European, ASTM, AOAC, FDA, ISO, EN, DIN, and NIOSH methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows include BS EN 1388 lead/cadmium release, EN 13130 specific migrations, EN 1186 overall migration, ASTM C738/C927, AOAC 973.32/973.82, 84/500/EEC, US FDA 21 CFR sections, ISO 15320, NIOSH 9100, EN 645/647, and EN 1541.

evidence.claim: US FDA food-contact extractives testing is within scope for multiple 21 CFR material categories, with some limitations noted.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows cite US FDA 21 CFR 176.170, 175.300, 177.1580, 177.1240, 177.1350 with only net chloroform-soluble extractives, 177.1210, 177.1460, 177.1520, 177.1630, 177.1640, 177.1810 except molecular weight and glass transition points, and other sections.

evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR non-lighting scope includes computer sleep, short idle, and off-mode tests under Program Requirements for Computers V8.0 and computer test methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows list ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Computers V8.0 and ENERGY STAR Test Method for Computer revisions October 2019 and Final Test Method Rev. July 2022 for sleep mode, short idle mode, and off mode.

evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR non-lighting scope includes display on-mode, luminance, standby, and off-mode testing with luminance accreditation limited to 25-1500 cd/m2 for listed display methods.
evidence.evidenceText: Display rows cite ENERGY STAR Program Requirements for Displays V8.0, ENERGY STAR display test method revisions, VESA DisplayHDR CTS Version 1.0, ICDM IDMS Version 1.03, CTA-2037-A, and note “Accredited only for (25-1500)cd/m2.”

evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR non-lighting scope includes water cooler testing under ENERGY STAR Program Requirements V3.0 and ANSI/ASHRAE 18-2008, with POU water cooler excluded.
evidence.evidenceText: Water cooler rows list on mode with no water draw, OMP for cold and hot water draw, cold-water dispenser capacity, and hot-water dispenser capacity; notes state “Except for: POU water cooler.”

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 18 of 18 and begins mid-table; title, issuing dates, laboratory identity, and certificate validity dates may appear in earlier chunks.

Source path references a router DHF package, but this chunk primarily covers toy safety, food contact materials, and ENERGY STAR non-lighting products; relevance to Archer C64/router regulatory evidence should be verified.
The document states that the Chinese accreditation scope remains definitive; English table text should be treated as an aid unless checked against the Chinese scope.
